choice: Branch model: merge PRs directly to `main` after clean testing/code-review; Railway auto-deploys on merge to `main` (no `dev` integration branch, no branch protection). Implement Playwright from the start rather than deferring it — UX is a core part of the solution.
rationale: Human-chosen during grooming (overrode recommendation: Use `main` = Railway-tracked release, `dev` = integration branch, promote `dev → main` to deploy (keeps Railway config untouched; `main`=production is the conventional model). Make the engine suite a blocking required check on PRs into `dev` if you'll accept that one bit of protection — it restores 'red can't reach the branch you promote from' without touching `main`; if you want zero protection anywhere, keep it advisory and treat the `dev→main` promotion as a deliberate look-then-ship. Either way, concentrate v1 spend on the engine spec suite and defer Playwright until visualization stabilizes.)
